sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἐπακολουθέω

- Transliteration: Epakoloutheo

- Phonetic: ep-ak-ol-oo-theh'-o

- Definition:

1. to follow (close) upon, follow after

2. to tread in one's footsteps i.e. to imitate his example

- Origin: "from G1909">1909) and 190"

- TDNT entry: 04:35,3

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G1909 and G190; to accompany: - follow (after).
